title: Capacity recommendation (Contract 7, fleetlab -> inferops)
description: Machine-checkable capacity recommendation closing the I6 feedback loop (Scenario E: benchmark results -> recommendation -> deployment change -> re-measurement). Normative structural rules: (1) inputs are explicit REFERENCES — a recommendation that does not name the benchmark results, workload version, SLO, cost profile, and hardware profiles it was derived from is not valid; (2) every predicted quantity carries STATED UNCERTAINTY — a point prediction without an interval is not expressible; (3) assumptions and sensitivity notes are required — empty is not an option, because a model without stated assumptions is a fabricated default.

inputs.description: Explicit references to everything the recommendation was derived from. These pins make the recommendation auditable and the re-measurement comparable.

benchmark_result_ids.description: result_id values of the benchmark-result artifacts the model was fitted to.

demand_forecast.description: The demand the recommendation is sized for.

basis.description: Where the forecast comes from (measured traffic, stated planning assumption, ...).

recommended_topology.description: The recommended serving topology: replica counts per hardware type plus the engine configuration they run. inferops applies this as an experiment (deployment-contract instances + manifests), never blindly.

flags.description: Engine runtime flags for this group (same vocabulary as benchmark-run.engine.flags, so the applied config is comparable to the measured one).

baseline.description: The topology the recommendation changes FROM (e.g. the measured single-replica baseline), making the recommended change explicit.

change_summary.description: One-line statement of the topology change (Scenario E evidence).

predictions.description: Predicted goodput, latency, and cost under the recommended topology — every quantity WITH stated uncertainty (structurally required).

at_offered_rps.description: Offered rate the goodput prediction is stated at (typically the demand forecast peak).

latency.description: Predicted latency statistics, keyed <signal>_<statistic> using Contract 2 signal names (e.g. ttft_seconds_p95, itl_seconds_p99, e2e_duration_seconds_p95).

cost.description: Predicted cost under the referenced cost profile.

autoscaling.description: Recommended autoscaling signal and thresholds. The signal comes from the Contract 2 vocabulary (source=gateway) or the backend-capability canonical keys (source=engine) — never engine-native metric names.

statistic.description: Aggregation over the signal (e.g. p95 over 1m) when it is a distribution.

assumptions.description: Every assumption the prediction rests on, stated explicitly (linear scaling, routing policy, workload stationarity, ...).

sensitivity_notes.description: Which inputs the recommendation is most sensitive to, and how it degrades when they move.

re_measurement.description: Plan for the re-measurement that closes the loop (Scenario E): re-run the referenced workload against the applied topology with the topology change as the single declared experimental variable (benchmark comparability rule).

predictedQuantity.description: A predicted value WITH stated uncertainty. The uncertainty interval is REQUIRED: a point prediction without one is not a valid recommendation input for an operational change.

method.description: How the interval was obtained (bootstrap over pooled events, stated model-error margin, ...). An interval without a method is a decoration, not an uncertainty statement.

for_seconds.description: Sustain duration before the action fires (flap damping).
